layout_description:
Option: OPT-1-REVISED
Slide description: TWO-COLUMN SIDE-BY-SIDE LAYOUT with equal-weight columns separated by a consistent 2% gutter. The slide uses a 4-column × 6-row grid structure with all containers snapping to grid boundaries. Both tables are RECREATE containers and receive identical visual treatment using Section 4 Option C (Flat Geometry Table) from the design guidelines — formal financial data tables with no filled header bars, accent tabs beneath column headers, light grey row label cells, and thin dashed grey separators between rows. The layout maximizes both horizontal and vertical space utilization: tables allocate roughly 40% and 46% width respectively with small margins at slide edges, and extend vertically from just below the title area to approximately 80% of slide height, filling 85–90% of the usable slide area. The slide uses content-aware spacing appropriate for dense tabular content: padding is minimal, row heights are generous enough to accommodate readable font sizes (medium body font range, 13–14pt), and dashed separator lines provide visual rhythm without consuming excessive vertical space. All row sections within each table use equal flexGrow to distribute height evenly across the full allocated container height, preventing top-stacking and ensuring the tables fill their containers completely. The layout enforces horizontal alignment precision: corresponding rows in both tables share identical top and bottom edges, and all separator lines align horizontally across both columns at matching vertical positions.

Blueprint:
  Orientation: left-to-right
  Regions: 2
  Allocation: [0.4, 0.46]

Containers:
  C0 (LEFT COLUMN — Bookings Table): Left column table occupying approximately 40% of slide width, positioned with a small left margin (3% from slide edge). Height extends from just below the title area (top_pct ~14%) to approximately 80% of slide height (~66% height allocation), filling the available vertical space completely. This is a RECREATE container using Section 4 Option C (Flat Geometry Table) visual treatment. The table has 7 columns (Region, Customer, Country, Q1, Jan, Feb, Mar) and 18 data rows organized into two regional sections (NA with ~10 rows including subtotal, Intl with ~8 rows including subtotal, plus a grand Total row). Column headers are plain bold text in brand dark color, left-aligned, with no filled background. Directly beneath each header label sits a small accent tab (brand primary color, very thin height approximately 2–3% of row height, narrow width approximately 20–25% of column width, top corners slightly rounded, left-aligned with the header text, sitting fully below the text baseline and never overlapping it). Immediately below and touching each accent tab is a thin solid horizontal line in brand dark color, spanning that column's width with small white gutters (1–2% of column width) between columns — not a single full-width line. The first column (Region) contains row label cells: light grey filled rectangles (no border, no rounded corners, full column width, occupying approximately 80% of the row height) with bold text in brand dark color, left-aligned with a small left inset (5% of column width), vertically centered. These label cells span multiple rows for each region (NA spans rows 1–10, Intl spans rows 11–18). Other columns contain numerical data: plain text on white background (no fill), brand text color, horizontally centered within each cell, vertically centered to align with the row label cell baseline. Rows are separated by thin dashed grey horizontal lines spanning the full table width. The 'Total NA' and 'Total Intl' rows receive bold text treatment to distinguish them as subtotals. The 'Total' row at the bottom receives bold text and sits below a slightly heavier dashed separator (1.5× the weight of standard row separators). Font size is medium body font in the 13–14pt range, ensuring all text fits comfortably within the expanded row heights without overflow while filling the allocated vertical space. All 18 row sections (including header row, 16 data rows, and total row) use equal flexGrow to distribute height evenly across the full 66% container height, preventing content from bunching at the top and ensuring the table bottom edge sits at 80% of slide height. Highlighted cells (green fills visible in source for specific Jan/Feb/Mar cells) preserve their exact highlight colors as accent fills behind the cell text. Column widths are content-weighted: Region column sized to fit 'Intl' text with small padding (~8% of table width), Customer column sized to fit longest customer name 'New US - 2026' without wrapping (~18% of table width), Country column narrow for 2-letter codes (~6% of table width), numerical columns (Q1, Jan, Feb, Mar) share remaining space equally (~17% each).
  C1 (RIGHT COLUMN — Revenues Table): Right column table occupying approximately 46% of slide width, positioned with a small right margin (3% from slide edge) and a 2% gutter separating it from the left column. Height extends from just below the title area (top_pct ~12.5%) to approximately 80% of slide height (~67.5% height allocation), matching the vertical extent and bottom edge position of the left column table. This is a RECREATE container using Section 4 Option C (Flat Geometry Table) visual treatment, identical to the left column. The table has 8 columns (Region, Customer, Country, Q1, Jan, Feb, Mar, and an unlabeled rightmost column visible in the source slide) and 18 data rows organized into two regional sections (NA with ~10 rows including subtotal, Intl with ~8 rows including subtotal, plus a grand Total row). The title text 'Revenues - 2026' visible in the source image is rendered as a bold header text row above the column headers, spanning the full table width, with brand dark text on white background (no filled bar), left-aligned, occupying approximately 8% of the table height. Column headers are plain bold text in brand dark color, left-aligned, with no filled background. Directly beneath each header label sits a small accent tab (brand primary color, very thin height approximately 2–3% of row height, narrow width approximately 20–25% of column width, top corners slightly rounded, left-aligned with the header text, sitting fully below the text baseline). Immediately below and touching each accent tab is a thin solid horizontal line in brand dark color, spanning that column's width with small white gutters (1–2% of column width) between columns. The first column (Region) contains row label cells: light grey filled rectangles (no border, no rounded corners, full column width, occupying approximately 80% of the row height) with bold text in brand dark color, left-aligned with a small left inset (5% of column width), vertically centered. These label cells span multiple rows for each region (NA spans rows 1–10, Intl spans rows 11–18). Other columns contain numerical data: plain text on white background, brand text color, horizontally centered within each cell, vertically centered to align with the row label cell baseline. Rows are separated by thin dashed grey horizontal lines spanning the full table width, positioned at exactly the same vertical positions as the separator lines in the left column table (horizontal alignment precision rule B5 — corresponding rows in both tables share identical top_pct and height_pct values). The 'Total NA' and 'Total Intl' rows receive bold text treatment. The 'Total' row at the bottom receives bold text and sits below a slightly heavier dashed separator (1.5× standard weight). Font size matches the left column exactly (within-slide font uniformity rule C6) — medium body font in the 13–14pt range, ensuring all text fits comfortably within the expanded row heights. All 18 row sections (including title row, header row, 16 data rows, and total row) use equal flexGrow values matching the left column, ensuring that corresponding data rows in both tables share identical top and bottom edges. The flexGrow distribution accounts for the additional title row in C1: title row receives proportionally less flexGrow (~0.8×) than data rows, so data row heights match C0 exactly. Highlighted cells (green fills visible in source for specific cells in the Mar column and other locations) preserve their exact highlight colors. Column widths are content-weighted: Region column sized to fit 'Intl' (~7% of table width), Customer column sized to fit longest name 'Thundercat (VA)' (~16% of table width), Country column narrow (~5%), numerical columns (Q1, Jan, Feb, Mar) share remaining space (~12% each), and the unlabeled rightmost column receives minimal width (~8%) sized to fit its content.
  SC-BOTTOM (DECORATIVE BACKGROUND LAYER): Inactive. The slide is well-filled by the two content tables, which together cover approximately 86–90% of the usable slide area (2 tables × 40–46% width × 66–68% height). No decorative background layer is needed — adding one would violate the accent color budget (rule C3) and provide no visual grouping benefit. The tables are visually unified through their identical design treatment (Flat Geometry Table style) and precise horizontal alignment of row separators.
  SC-TOP (OVERLAY CONNECTOR LAYER): Inactive. No cross-container connectors, arrows, or overlay elements are needed. The two tables are independent data presentations with no semantic relationship requiring visual connection. All content is contained within the two table containers.

_reference_violations.description: C1 (Revenues Table): Multiple cells have content placed in the wrong columns. In the NA section, row 'WashU' shows '129 2' in the Jan column instead of '129' in Jan and '2' in Feb. Row 'JGH' shows '129 2' in Jan instead of '129' in Jan and '2' in Feb. Row 'McGill' shows '543 8' in Feb instead of '543' in Feb and '8' in Mar. In the Intl section, row 'Hadassah' shows '366 3' in Jan instead of '366' in Jan and '3' in Feb. Row 'Assuta' shows '104 7' in Jan instead of '104' in Jan and '7' in Feb. This represents systematic column transposition errors where adjacent cell values are merged into a single cell.

C1 (Revenues Table): The sketch shows only 8 data rows in the Intl section (Manchester, Netcare, Albert Einstein, Hadassah, Assuta, Ichilov, Clalit, Sheba) but the source slide shows 9 data rows in the Intl section. The row 'Sheba' appears to be missing from the sketch's Intl section, reducing the total data row count from 18 to 17.

C0 and C1: The two content containers visibly overlap. The right edge of the left table (C0 - Bookings) extends beyond its allocated boundary and overlaps with the left edge of the right table (C1 - Revenues). The layout description does not explicitly permit overlapping containers, so any visible overlap is a critical violation.

C0 and C1: The corresponding rows in both tables do not share identical top edges and heights as required by the layout description's horizontal alignment precision rule. Visual inspection shows that row separators in the left table do not align horizontally with corresponding row separators in the right table at matching vertical positions.

_reference_critique:
C1 (Revenues Table) has critical content fidelity failures:

1. COLUMN TRANSPOSITION ERRORS: Multiple rows show cell content merged or placed in wrong columns. Specifically:
   - NA section, WashU row: '129 2' appears in Jan column; must be '129' in Jan, '2' in Feb
   - NA section, JGH row: '129 2' appears in Jan column; must be '129' in Jan, '2' in Feb  
   - NA section, McGill row: '543 8' appears in Feb column; must be '543' in Feb, '8' in Mar
   - Intl section, Hadassah row: '366 3' appears in Jan column; must be '366' in Jan, '3' in Feb
   - Intl section, Assuta row: '104 7' appears in Jan column; must be '104' in Jan, '7' in Feb

   SVGSketcher must parse the source data cell-by-cell and place each value in its correct column position. Each cell must occupy exactly one column. Do not merge adjacent cell values into a single cell.

2. MISSING ROW: The Intl section shows only 8 data rows but source has 9. The row 'Sheba' with values (IL, 118, 104, 7, 7) appears to be missing from the Intl section. SVGSketcher must include all 18 data rows from the source slide.

3. CONTAINER OVERLAP: C0 and C1 visibly overlap — the right edge of C0 extends into C1's left boundary. CSS_layout_agent must recalculate container coordinates: C0 should occupy left_pct=3%, width_pct=40%, C1 should occupy left_pct=45% (3% + 40% + 2% gutter), width_pct=46%, ensuring a clean 2% gutter with no overlap.

4. ROW ALIGNMENT FAILURE: Corresponding rows in C0 and C1 do not share identical top edges. CSS_layout_agent must ensure that each data row in C0 and its corresponding row in C1 have identical top_pct and height_pct values so that all horizontal separator lines align across both tables at matching vertical positions.
